intro.headline: Almost nothing is permanently missable — but the Prologue has a few one-shot moments.
points: After the story you can free-roam and clean up the vast majority of side content, so don't stress about exploring later.
The real missables are concentrated in the Prologue in Lumière, before the Harbor point-of-no-return.
When you approach the Harbor as Sophie you get a selectable prompt — pick "No" if you still have things to grab.
This list focuses on the genuinely time-sensitive things so you can progress confidently.

sections.title: Prologue — do these before the Harbor (genuinely missable)
sections.note: Lumière during the festival is a one-time window. Approaching the Harbor ends it (it's a selectable action, so you can back out).

items.label: Fight the Mime in Lumière before heading to the Harbor
Grab the Old Key during the festival scene to open the gate to the Journal
Fully explore festival-era Lumière before approaching the Harbor as Sophie

sections.title: Time-sensitive actions (don't skip these)
sections.note: These are tied to specific story beats — easy to walk past if you're rushing.

items.label: Don't kill Nevrons before completing their associated quests
Check up on the others at camp after the events at Visages
With Maelle, choose the "Yes. (Truth)" option to raise her relationship from 6 to 7
Heed the point-of-no-return prompt before the final act and finish outstanding side content first

sections.title: Return-friendly content (clean up at your pace)
sections.note: None of this is lost by progressing — track it whenever you like.

items.label: Music Records / Journals across the world map
Optional boss fights and Chromatic / superbosses
Pictos collected from exploration and enemies
Esquie ability upgrades that open new areas to revisit
Relationship / camp conversations with each Expeditioner
